content.full_text:
California
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornmission
March 2■′ ■990
Steve Pedrazzi
President
Board of Directors
Patterson Hospital District
Del Puerto Hospital
P. O. Box 187
Patterson, cA 953 63
Re3 Xi;.,ff"n";:: f5.:li;"ar 
Assistance
Dear Mr. Pedrazzi:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regard-
ing the duties and responsibil ities of ur. walter Rea, Sr., under
the confl ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the rrActtr) .1 You have not stated in your letter that !fr. Rea has
authorized your request for advice. Accordingly, we consider your
Ietter to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to ReguLa-
tion 18329(c) (copy enclosed. )2 we do not provide advice concern-
ing laws other than the Act. Please consult with the office of
the california Attorney ceneral regarding the doctrine of
incornpatible public offices.
OUESTIONS
1. Do the confL ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Act
disgualify Mr. Rea from participating in decisions regarding
certain real property presently owned by the Patterson Hospital
Di strict?
2. Do the confl ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Act
disgualify Mr. Rea frorn speaking publicJ.y on issues related to the
disposition or use of the real property in question?
1 Go.r".rrr"nt Code Sections 81OOO-91015. AI] statutory references
are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Infornal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
innunity provided by an opinion or formal written advj,ce. (Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329 (c) . )
428 J Street,Suite 800 ● P.o.Box 807 0 Sacramento CA
95804‐0807 ● (916)322・5660


FiLe No. I-9 0-13 7
Page 2
3. Are the offices of hospital district 
board member and
president of a county commission on aging incornpatible offices?
CONCLUSIONS
1. The conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Act do not
disqualify l,!r. Rea fro:u participating in decisions regardinq real
property owned by the district.
2. The conflict-of-j,nterest provisions of the Act do not
disqualify l,!r. Rea frorn speaking publicly on issues related to the
disposition or use of the real property in question.
3. we do not provide advice concerning laws other than the
Act. Pfease consult with the office of the California Attorney
ceneral regarding the doctrine of incoupatible public offices.
FACTS
You are the President of the Board of Directors of the
Paterson Hospital District (the district). 
In that capacity, it
is your duty to advise other members of the board concerning their
duties and responsibilities under the Act. You seek our advice to
detenoine rrhether Mr. walter Rea, sr., nay partj.cipate in certain
decisions related to a parcel of real property olrned by the
distr ict.
Hospital districts are emposrered to hold property, exercise
euinent donain, operate health facilities, 
and do any and all acts
to carry out the local hospital district 
la!r. (Health and safety
Code Section 3272!. ) The purpose of the local hospital district
1aw is to protect the public health and welfare by furnishing
hospital facilities 
and health care services.
Ur. Rea is a nenber of the board of the district. 
He is also
President of the Stanislaus county Corornission on Aging.3 The du-
ties and powers of loca1 commissioners on aging are set forth in
welfare and fnstitutions code section 9351.
3 Foa the purposes of the Act, inco[e does not include:
Salary and reiDburseDent for expenses or per
diem received from a state, 1ocaI, or federal
government agency and reirnbursement for traveL
expenses and per diem received fron a bona fide
educational, acadenic, or charitable organization.
section 82030 (b) (2).
Thus, incone received from the county Commission on Aging, if any,
does not create a disqualifying financial interest.


File No. f-9 o- 13 7
Page 3
The district 
owns a parcel of real property adjacent to the
district,s 
hospltal facilities. 
The district 
sought to develop a
nedical office complex on the parcel, and applied to the City of
Patterson for zoning approval. The city denied approval.
During the public neeting of the city council at which the
project was considered, Mr. Rea, who opposed the project, offered
to buy the parcel for 55.00 Dore than the best offer the hospital
uight receive. He further offered to donate the real property to
the city provided the city would comnit to the use of the subject
property for a senior citizen center. Attached to your reguest
for advice is a transcript of Mr. Rea's statements at the city
council neeting.
Local efforts are currently under Iray by private citizens to
overturn the city's refusal to approve the project through
circulation of a ballot title 
and sunmary pursuant to Elections
Code Sections 4001-4011. fn response to these efforts, Ir{r. Rea
has continued to speak out publicly against the district's
proposed project. 
Pending the outcone of the public petition,
the district 
rnay be required to consider further action related to
the project.
ANALYSTS
Section 87100 prohibits a public official 
frou nak j,ng,
participating in, or using his or her official position to influ-
ence a governmental decision in which the official 
knohrs or has
reason to know he or she has a financial interest. 
Menbers of the
board of a trospital district are public officials. 
(Section
82048. )
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision rril1 have a naterial
financial effect, distingnrishable from its effect on the public
generally, on the official or a nember of his or her imrnediate
fanily or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investnent worth
one thousand dollars (91,000) or Dore.
(b) Any real property in vhich the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars (S1,000) or nore.
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a conmercial lending
institution in the regular course of busineis on
terns available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating trro hundred fifty 
do1-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, recaived


File No. I-90-137
Page 4
by or prornised to the public official within 12
months prior to the tine when the decision is nade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.
(e) Any donor of, or any internediary or agent
for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars (9250) or Dore in value
provided to, received by, or prornised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the tirne when
the decision is nade.
Section 87103 (a) -(e) .
You have not indicated in your request for advice whether
Llr. Rea has a financial interest which night disqualify hin fron
participating in decisions regarding the hospital district. 
You
have only indicated that Ur. Rea offered to purchase a parcel of
Iand orrned by the hospital district. 
It appears from your letter
that Ur. Rea did not purchase the property. You aLso indicate
that IrIr. Rea has expressed his opposition to the proposed roedical
office conplex. These facts are not disgualifying. 
Absent a
financial interest, Mr. Rea nay participate freely in all natters
concerning the district, 
including decisions regarding the real
property at issue.
Should you have any further guestions regarding this natter,
do not hesitate to contact ne at (916) 322-5901.
sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
ceneral Counsel
